D5 Design L3 Apply Reference

Complementary Contrast

opposite hues sharpen each other side by side — yet average to a dead grey when mixed, the proof that they are complements

A complementary pair sits directly across the wheel. Placed side by side, each makes the other look more intense — the eye craves the balance. But average their light and they cancel to neutral grey. Intensification apart, annihilation together: that is the signature of true complements.

Choose a hue; its complement appears beside it. The two large fields intensify one another. The centre strip is their optical average — for a true complementary pair it lands on neutral grey, neither hue winning.
